blurb: Tough native prairie grass named for the oat-like seeds dangling along one side of each arching stem; flashes bright orange-red anthers at bloom and warms to bronze in fall.
size: 1.5–2.5 ft tall in seed × 1.5 ft wide
spread: Clumping to slowly spreading by short rhizomes
seasons: Blue-green in summer with one-sided seed spikes and orange anthers; bronze-purple fall; tan winter
wildlife: Larval host for several skippers; seeds feed birds; warm-season prairie cover
